Mentions Timeline (7 articles)
3310-08-15
Utopia Fails to Reinforce Research Archive Library
Utopia's campaign in Polevnic ends without reaching its quota, with Antal thanking participants and Yong-Rui offering to fill the supply gap.
3310-08-08
Utopia Requests Support Amid Claims of Sirius Pressure
Pranav Antal asks pilots to deliver research materials to Polevnic, claiming Sirius Corporation is pressuring Utopia's suppliers and threatening the Sim Archive's data integrity.
3304-12-28
Antal Levels Accusation at Vitadyne
Simguru Pranav Antal, leader of Utopia, accuses Vitadyne Labs of stealing nanomedicine technology developed in Utopia decades ago. Antal claims a medical transport disappeared from the Polevnic system months ago and that Professor Katrien Rook acquired the stolen cargo. Utopia demands the return of its property. The IHO announces it will investigate the accusation.
3301-07-07
Memorandum of Understanding between citizens of Utopia and Sirius Corp
Utopian and Sirius Gov commanders agreed a non-aggression pact recognising each other's right to exist and allowing free travel within each other's space. Freelance commanders can sign the pact in Lembava or Polevnic. The agreement aims to promote trade and ensure continued prosperity in both independent territories.
3301-06-12
Winters Sends Envoys to Utopia
Emissaries of Shadow President Felicia Winters were spotted meeting Utopian representatives aboard Tanner Settlement in Polevnic. A spokesman dismissed it as routine, but pundits speculate the visit could signal support for sim gaming or a metals trade deal, given Utopia's large order of assorted metals for delivery to Antal. Winters' outreach to Utopia contrasts with the previous administration's hard-line stance against sim addiction.
3301-06-06
A Vision of the Future
Utopia's compound on Antal has long displayed technology years ahead of its time. Under Simguru Pranav Antal, the Utopian Commune Programme expanded to Polevnic, Neris, HIP 117972, and HIP 1389. Hundreds of thousands of scientists and engineers have joined, developing new technologies for mass production. Utopia requires approximately 30 million tonnes of assorted metals monthly and offers bonuses to traders who deliver large supplies to Lobachevsky Station in Antal.
3301-06-06
Galaxy's Most Powerful People – Simguru Pranav Antal
Profile of Simguru Pranav Antal, heir to Rishi Antal's legacy of transcendental technology. Despite predictions that Utopia would collapse without its founder, Pranav expanded the Utopian Commune Programme to multiple systems. He told GalNet that imposing order through innovation and understanding is key to humanity's happiness, and that mastering oneself is necessary to master the worlds around us.
